title: Request Authority, Host, Target Reconstruction, and Routing
description: A server trusts any Host, accepts duplicate authority sources, reconstructs links from forwarded input, routes before validating the target, and confuses TLS identity with request authority. Learners cumulatively produce authority validation and target reconstruction table through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Require exactly one valid Host field for HTTP/1.1 where applicable and reject missing, empty, duplicate, or invalid authority.
Reconstruct the effective request URI from target form, request-target, Host, connection security, and trusted configuration.
Handle proxy absolute-form authority and Host according to RFC rules while rejecting unsafe disagreement.
